file.content:
do print("testing 'format %a %A'")
  local function matchhexa (n)
    local s = string.format("%a", n)
    -- result matches ISO C requirements
    assert(string.find(s, "^%-?0x[1-9a-f]%.?[0-9a-f]*p[-+]?%d+$"))
    assert(tonumber(s) == n)  -- and has full precision
    s = string.format("%A", n)
    assert(string.find(s, "^%-?0X[1-9A-F]%.?[0-9A-F]*P[-+]?%d+$"))
    assert(tonumber(s) == n)
  end
  for _, n in ipairs{0.1, -0.1, 1/3, -1/3, 1e30, -1e30,
                     -45/247, 1, -1, 2, -2, 3e-20, -3e-20} do
    matchhexa(n)
  end

  assert(string.find(string.format("%A", 0.0), "^0X0%.?0*P%+?0$"))
  assert(string.find(string.format("%a", -0.0), "^%-0x0%.?0*p%+?0$"))

  if not _port then   -- test inf, -inf, NaN, and -0.0
    assert(string.find(string.format("%a", 1/0), "^inf"))
    assert(string.find(string.format("%A", -1/0), "^%-INF"))
    assert(string.find(string.format("%a", 0/0), "^%-?nan"))
    assert(string.find(string.format("%a", -0.0), "^%-0x0"))
  end
  
  if not pcall(string.format, "%.3a", 0) then
    (Message or print)("\n >>> modifiers for format '%a' not available <<<\n")
  else
    assert(string.find(string.format("%+.2A", 12), "^%+0X%x%.%x0P%+?%d$"))
    assert(string.find(string.format("%.4A", -12), "^%-0X%x%.%x000P%+?%d$"))
  end
end


-- testing some flags  (all these results are required by ISO C)
assert(string.format("%#12o", 10) == "         012")
assert(string.format("%#10x", 100) == "      0x64")
assert(string.format("%#-17X", 100) == "0X64             ")
assert(string.format("%013i", -100) == "-000000000100")
assert(string.format("%2.5d", -100) == "-00100")
assert(string.format("%.u", 0) == "")
assert(string.format("%+#014.0f", 100) == "+000000000100.")
assert(string.format("%-16c", 97) == "a               ")
assert(string.format("%+.3G", 1.5) == "+1.5")
assert(string.format("%.0s", "alo")  == "")
assert(string.format("%.s", "alo")  == "")

-- ISO C89 says that "The exponent always contains at least two digits",
-- but unlike ISO C99 it does not ensure that it contains "only as many
-- more digits as necessary".

4	# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
5	# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
6	# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
7	# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
8	# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure

41	COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
42	OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"
43	
44	PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
45	_soft = true
46	_port = true
47	_nomsg = true
48	_U = false
49	arg = arg or {}
50	_G = _G or _ENV
51	if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
52	'
53	
54	{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"
55	
56	TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
57	export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"
58	
59	run_with_timeout() {
60	    local src_file="$1"
61	    local out_file="$2"
62	    local src
63	    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
64	    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
65	        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
66	    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
67	        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
68	    else
69	        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
70	        local pid=$!
71	        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
72	        local watcher=$!
73	        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
74	        local rc=$?
75	        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
76	        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
77	        return $rc
78	    fi
79	}
80	
81	run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true
82	
83	reached_exec=0
84	grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1
85	
86	if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
87	    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then

89	    exit 0
90	fi

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1
